title: Black Hole Jet Power Measured | Cygnus X-1 First Direct Measurement
subtitle: 18 years of radio imaging data yielded the first direct measurement of a black hole jet's instantaneous power: equivalent to 10,000 suns.
content_html:
Astronomers made the first direct measurement of a black hole jet's instantaneous power, finding Cygnus X-1's jets carry energy equivalent to 10,000 suns at speeds exceeding 150,000 km per second.

The Measurement Method

A Curtin University-led team analyzed 18 years of radio imaging data from Cygnus X-1, the first confirmed black hole, discovered in the 1970s. The study was published in Nature Astronomy in April 2026.

Why This Matters for Galaxy Evolution

Black hole jets are believed to regulate star formation across entire galaxies. Measuring their power directly for the first time gives astrophysicists a calibration point for models of galaxy evolution going back to the early universe.
